mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates eliminate short-term discount rate pressure on equity valuations, generating consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns as capital seeks yield in equities.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical edge (Rank IC < 0.02) when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 60% of instances where UNRATE drops > 0.2pp, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (accuracy < 55%) or Rank IC remains below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contractions during late-cycle expansions trigger immediate profit margin concerns, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns within one week as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of INDPRO contraction months during late-cycle phases, invalidating the recession pricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly supports corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive equity market returns over one month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation exceeding 2.5 percent annually forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% YoY, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm genuine labor market strength rather than statistical artifacts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 58% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during high-inflation regimes trigger immediate negative equity returns as markets price in supply chain disruptions and margin compression.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of INDPRO decline months during high-inflation periods, or Rank IC stays below 0.02.
